description_long: This object is a historical flintlock or percussion cap shotgun, featuring a long, dark wooden stock with a smooth, curved grip and a single, elongated metal barrel. The barrel is metallic, likely steel or iron, and is affixed to the wooden stock with visible metal fastenings. The hammer and trigger mechanism are exposed, with the hammer positioned above the trigger and a rounded trigger guard below. The overall design is streamlined, with minimal ornamentation, and the wood shows a polished, slightly worn finish. The muzzle is cylindrical and the barrel appears to be of uniform diameter, with no visible ribbing or venting. The shotgun's compact size and classic styling suggest it is an antique or reproduction suitable for display or period reenactment.
description: A classic, antique-style shotgun with a long metal barrel and a polished wooden stock, featuring an exposed hammer and a rounded trigger guard. The design is simple, streamlined, and historically inspired.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the shotgun, highlighting the curved wooden grip, long metal barrel, and exposed hammer and trigger guard.
description_view_1: Top-down view emphasizing the narrow width of the barrel and stock, with the hammer and trigger mechanism partially visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, displaying the same features from the other side, including the smooth wooden stock and metal barrel.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view focusing on the underside of the stock and barrel, showing the rounded grip and the alignment of the barrel with the stock.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, no visible ribbing or venting, uniform barrel length
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard with a smooth, continuous curve and no finger grooves or reinforcement
